text: We present the case of a thirty-four-year-old man who came to our attention for an incidental localized thirty-three millimeter renal mass in the hindmus of our shoe kidney. The right kidney had three arteries. The assessment of the CT angiogram suggests that the mass was fed by the lower right renal artery.

The surgical team performs a robot-assisted partial nephrectomy using the DaVinci XI system in a forearm configuration. The patient is positioned supine with the right flank elevated at twenty degrees and a thirty-degree Trendelenburg tilt. Four 8mm robotic ports and two 12mm assistant ports are placed. The ascending colon is mobilized, exposing the isthmus of the osteokidney and the renal tumor.
The surgeon prepares the three right renal arteries, carefully isolating each artery from surrounding tissue using robotic instruments.

The surgeon clamps the lower right renal artery using a bulldog clamp and injects indocyanine green intravenously. The Firefly fluorescence imaging confirms the devascularization of the renal mass, which emits green fluorescence upon restoration of blood flow. The edges of the renal tumor are assessed with an ultrasound probe, and the incision line is scored on the renal parenchyma.
Following definitive clamping, the surgeon uses cold scissors to resect the renal mass under selective ischemia. The excised mass is then placed into an endo bag for removal.

The surgeon performs medullary sutures in a running fashion using a 3-0 monocryl stitch, securing the edges with Hem-o-lok clips. Following unclamping, a cortical suture is executed in a running fashion using a V-Loc suture with a sliding clip technique.
Surgiflo hemostatic agent is applied to the renal bridge, followed by the placement of a tubular drain in the surgical site.

The operative time and consult time was one hundred sixty-five minutes and one hundred twenty-five minutes respectively. Warming ischemia time was nine minutes. The estimated blood loss was one hundred milliliters. Post-operative serum creatinine was normal. No post-operative complication occurred and the patient was discharged in the third post-operative day. Pathology show a clear cell renal carcinoma, PT-Ia, with negative surgical margins.
